park_name: BIG4 Kryal Castle Holiday Park - Ballarat

reviews.review_text: Great facilities. Great and friendly staff. Great location overlooking Ballarat. Easy access from freeway and easy to get to all sites to see around Ballarat. Highly recommend if in the Ballarat region

We stayed here friday and checked out today.
We stayed in a tiny home and that was amazing, so nice and clean. Tanya was very amazing when we needed anything. My son enjoyed patting the nice horse in the paddock, i wish we knew the name he wanted to ride it. Had unusual eye colour. We also loved the abbey tavern for breakfast, and the castle grounds we had the most amazing time. All the staff were super friendly and they all deserve such praise.

The games area was a hit. We really wanna stay in a kings room with spa next time.

10/10 will stay again and highly recommend.

Suggestion would maybe in a little play area for kids until big 4 area. But definitelyl loved our stay

We had our caravan, thank goodness it's big, roomy & comfortable. The weather was so miserable with rain constant wind & the temperature did not get above 11 degrees for the 4 days we were there.
I know the weather is beyond anyone's control but the Big 4 could have had better facilities to accommodate the weather.  For $72 per night for a site, i would have expected a more accommodating camp kitchen. It was so small & uninviting no warmth. The deck was so wet & not usable. There is no pool or spas, no play ground. Very dismal.
Kryal castle, so confusing. We were not given clear information about how the costings worked
When i asked  the young bloke on the desk didn't have much experience. I wasn't paying anymore!
The toilet seats were loose, as if people had been standing/ squatting on them. They were not as clean as they could have been.
Very disappointing for a Big4. We won't be going back.

EDIT-  sadly toilets are still filthy - no cleaning or emptying  1.15 pm . 😟We are staying on a caravan site - toilets and showers were disgusting- poo on floor , toilet seats half hanging off, water all over floors , toilet paper on floors , bins full and you have to shower with a toilet needless to say with will wash with caravan  sink . we have  two nights here and decided to buy a bucket.  Caravan space was not the best soggy grass for caravan .Not happy . Cleaning of these facilities shoukd be done regularly not every 24 hours - especially for Big  4.
Overall, the accommodation was great and I had a pleasant stay. However, the shower head could be improved, as the water pressure was quite low and made it less enjoyable to use.

A quiet park with a great view. Small park with no pool or playground but you do have the option to buy a pass to have access to the castle everyday. Amenities are good and small camp kitchen and bbq were also kept well.

Went camping there for a night over the weekend. I HIGHLY recommend; the grass is so soft, sites are spacious and you get this awesome view. Amenities are surprisingly modern, just a little bit cramped in the kitchen. But hard to find your way around at first though. The reception is actually IN Kyral Castle, which took a while to find haha…

Issue with house keeping,
We  were staying in a cabin , I had moved the mattress to make sleeping more kid friendly,  18 month old twins, one with special needs.
A male house keeper after looking in said to his colleagues, in a derogatory tone that "they've made the room there own " then said "I should take photos to show the manager" (I was in the room at the time).
Issue was resolved eventually, but I found the episode distasteful as we were paying guests.
Another minor point, we only received one con. Breakfast instead of two.
PS. Not a word from the manager

Very poor experience and poor communication from the staff. No mention that we were able to use the castle after hours and that we needed to go into the castle to find a reception building, so we wasted a day and half at the park because we had no idea it was open… no map of caravan park to find our site, just a text message saying here’s the gate code, here’s your site number - good luck I guess? We were originally given a drive through site with no room to open our awning for a 3 night stay. Moved to another site which we didn’t fit on as sites are small and poor designed, so had to take up 2 sites. The castle was great but stay somewhere else

Comfortable stay in a tiny home over the weekend. Great to have our own bathroom, kitchen and little living area. Admission to Kryal Castle Park included in booking was great. Booking was taken over the phone by the lovely Tanya, who was very informative. Renee was also very helpful on arrival.
Potion class was an added cost and totally worth it. Herb the magical potion making wizard was hilarious and an absolute delight.
Ducky did amazing facepainting and was great with the kids.
Lady Renee and Sir Phillips jousting was exciting (Congratulations Lady Renee) and the whole show from the announcer to the jesters was brilliant and very interactive.
A really enjoyable and memorable weekend.
Thanks to all.

We went for a long weekend tent camping, the site was plenty big enough for a 10p tent and Tarp shelter plus our suv.
There is no play equipment or spaces for kids to play, just be aware if you're travelling with kids. But you might be able to access the castle to play on their equipment, just not as convenient if you need little people to buzz off for a few minutes as you need to accompany them.
The bathrooms are small but clean and quite new. They have powerpoints which was good as i brought my hair-dryer. The lights and fan are on a timer so they kept turning off while we showered but that wasn't too bad.
The camp kitchen is basic; fridge/freezer, microwave, single sink, stove and oven. Minimal bench space and small kitchen so i wouldn't want to try and use it at a busy time, you'd be waiting hours. We packed our own stove and small appliances, using only the sink late at night to do dishes when everyone was gone. There was also a bbq on the deck at the lichen too. No cutlery, a few utensils and no crockery for campers to use so bring what you need.
It was super quiet when we were there, but it seemed to be popular with caravans and campervans.
The staff were lovely, really helpful and assisted with a few booking issues we had.
Kryal castle on the door step made for a great day out and an easy walk to the tent.
Well in the 2 yrs since we stayed the place has not improved except for the cabins which are fine for a couple
Enjoyed our stay and had free access to parts of the castle. Breakfast was a nice touch and the staff, especially Ebony were very welcoming and looked after us very well. We would stay again.

The experience of the park itself is wonderful and timeless but the accommodation was less than impressive.
We were made to pay $30 extra even though we booked a queen room and did not specify two people would be staying instead of one.
Rooms are freezing cold even with the heater on 31 degrees.
The breakfast buffet was misleading and only had bread, mini cereals and very few pastries.
If you wanted a real breakfast, you had to pay $12 a plate for a measly portion of eggs, hash brown and bacon, might i add the dining hall was also painfully cold.
I suggest you come dressed in Mt Bula attire if you want to be comfortable.

Not a good place to stay, unless you want to sleep with flies and be frozen.

They would not tell you in advance what to expect in the room even though they knew flies would be everywhere in your room. Instead they will BLAME you for not knowing what to expect in countryside and not asking for help at night time. Well, I would have called the after hours services if I was told that they could help me with catching flies but no I wasn't told anything about what to expect and how to deal with it when things happened. Usually accommodations in regional areas would tell us what to expect like there could be wildlife around and what to do but not for this place. They said they would have sprayed something in my room if I had called them but I had a baby with me. I dont know what makes them think this would be a good solution. Most staff are nice but the management sounds arrogant.

Plus, the floor was greasy and sticky; windowsills were full of dust; we saw hair on a towel and bedsheet as well; the bathroom was freezing and none of us bothered to take a shower.

Also, dont book directly with them. It is better to book through a third party booking agent as they wouldnt get back to you when things happened even they said they would. At least the agent can help you follow things up. I had to try different channels to get someone calling me back!

We arrived after 5pm and followed instructions to collect the keys for our cabin, easy.
The cabin was very cold and despite heater , on for whole night never reached a really warm temperature . As the heater is high on the wall I suspect a lot of heat went out through the ceiling .
A big disappointment was there were No extra Blankets . We had to use bath towels and our coats on the bed to try to get warm .
There's also no hair-dryer in the bathroom !
All the above being said .
The staff are so pleasant, helpful and happy to chat about Castle and local places to visit.
We very much enjoyed the castle and all within  and in spring or summer will try to visit again, maybe bring family too.

We stayed in one of the tiny houses. It was what it said on the tin - tiny. They shake, you bang your head... But are still better appointed than other places I've stayed.

Good thing there's a castle nearby. It looks cool from the outside, but is a tourist trap selling you cheap plastic swords and food that will end up with wasps all over it.

The kids like it, though. And the jousting is fun.

We stayed at Kryal Castle holiday camp on the Easter break using our camper van, when we arrived, somebody was already in our space so we had a free upgrade which was nice 👍 unfortunately we were placed right near to the camp kitchen. The people down there were loud, noisy, screaming kids and had no regard for other people staying in the camp! The noise did not die down until gone past 10pm! This morning they had a birthday party celebration early in the morning! Look, I’m all for people having a good time, but I had 2 small children trying to sleep, nothing to do with the park, but some people are just ignorant of sharing with others! 😠 P.S facilities were great, new toilet and shower block, great views, Kryal Castle on your doorstep.

The location and services are really good. But when we checked in, there were many fine hairs on the bed sheets and pillows. I called the service staff over. He was very nice and brought me new bed sheets, but still couldn't solve the problem because there were even a lot of hairs on the quilt. That bed was simply uninhabitable! But I still want to thank the service staff of that day. He was very nice. Even when it got dark, he still came to our room to visit us!

Unfortunately the site still felt like a building site, there were sharp objects around the tiny home, definitely not safe for small kids to be around. Very narrow paths for cars to get to the homes due to building works, lucky we had a 4WD, I would hate to get there with a small car and navigate the pot holes. The air conditioning in the room could not keep up with the heat, the homes were hot and we were struggling to go to sleep. The skylights are a nice idea until you wake up every morning because it’s so bright as there are no blinds. Overall I would suggest to finish building works and upgrade the air conditioning in the rooms before charging $300 a night.
On a positive note the castle itself was lots of fun and the kids really enjoyed the atmosphere and entertainment

I did not like the tiny homes,
They are not fit even for gnomes.
Construction poor, and pieces chipped,
Is it to this that they have slipped?
The scary stairs and ladder steep
give added fear when to bed we creep.
Great value inclusive of cost of  entry into Kryal Castle with great shows & entertainment for my 3 & 5yo grandchildren.

Enjoyed the accommodation. Clean. A novel experience something different and interesting.  The history of the era was fascinating and enjoyed reading the visual displays.
The staff were very professional, polite and friendly. Ebony in the cafe was just lovely. Reception staff very welcoming.
Didn’t know much about the history of Kryal Castle, so looked up on Google. A suggestion we would like to put forward is to have a brochure in the rooms with history of Kryal Castle.

Location is amazing somewhere different to stay inside a castle! We stayed in a budget friendly room inside the castle grounds. The castle entertainment and attractions were great, even with our stay being a rainy one.

Staff were nice on check out, and late check in was made easy with staff setting up for me to collect room keys from the locker along with a map to my room.

One of the really good perks is having after hours access to the grounds to explore the castle without everyone there. The kids absolutely love this.

The room however, I expected basic but neat accommodation as pictured. It was a little run down, big strip's of paint coming away from the ceiling in the bathroom quite heavily. Puttied holes in walls, stains on cabinets and spills on walls. The door to our room couldn't close properly without alot of messing with so I used the door stopper wedge at night to make sure it was closed.

I understand I booked basic accommodation, I didn't think it would be a mold and possible lead paint risk.

If I were to stay again I would spend more to stay in they're brand new tiny homes/cabins they have on the property instead which do look very nice.
Next time might be a day trip.
Very reasonably priced, clean cabin. Had everything we needed. Will definitely be back!

We attended a wedding and stayed in a King room. The room was clean and spacious with everything at your doorstep.
A great night spent with family and friends. Thank you

Our room was roomy clean and felt like we were back in medieval times. The staff were friendly helpful and had good knowledge of activities in the local area.  The jousting, stores and activities were fun
We had a ball! Love this place
Interesting place to stay....not your average everyday motel which is a good thing!
The room was really comfortable.
Loved our stay. The staff were so friendly and helpful. Would definitely stay again.
Great for families! Wonderful accommodation and customer service!

Black mold over bathroom ceiling. $30 for a cot that had no mattress so couldn't use. Bunk beds were rock hard and king bed was 2 singles pushed together. Some shop staff were awesome while others quite rude and unfriendly. Great entertainment from the knights, kids loved the animals. Would visit again but wouldn't stay there and would choose different accommodation.
Perfect place to visit and stay. We stay at family room 6 people. Very cheap but comfortable.
Room was very clean.  Was a cold night and reverse cycle heating was on for our arrival.  Very good service.

Overall happy with the clean, new and modern 2 person cabin. The shower head should be addressed though as did not give the desired outcome a shower should. Could do with some hooks to hang a jacket etc. liked the fact you have access to the castle without paying extra. Will be back, thank you

I would like to thank the staff for all their help and compassion making our stay very memorable.  The room was perfect size as mum uses a walker,  The things we got to see and do were perfect for the elderly.
We loved the time we were there. Next trip we can focus on the events.

Our Stay at Kryal Castle was more than we expected . our room was excellent Quality Bedding and Towels  But most of all was the staff that attended to 2 Little emergencies and were so helpful to get us back on the road again .I didn't get your names but you know who you are A great big thanks Love you girls 🙏👍

Unfortunately the cabin was extremely tiny. There wasn’t anywhere to even put our bags. The shower had no pressure and it was so hard to even wash the shampoo out of my hair. Also there was a toaster and plate but no knife so if we made toast we couldn’t butter it. The camp kitchen also had nothing to cook with so no way of cooking our dinner. The idea is there but just not quite pulled off.
The view at night was stunning though, unfortunately there was no chairs on the deck to sit and enjoy it

We had a fantastic stay at Kryal Castle! The kids loved living in a castle for a couple of days! The staff were amazing, very kind and kept in character.  It was a fantastic experience we will remember forever.

Staff was nice and helpful.  I received email reminders, SMS and phone call to make sure everything could go smoothly.  Lived inside the castle was also a wonderful and unique experience. HOWEVER, the room had absolutely NO noises isolation, our next door got a new born baby who needed to get up several times to feed and we could hear everything happened all night.  I don't blame the baby or anyone about it.  But the room itself was really cheap.  I like the castle and I will come again but I will find a different place to live overnight.

Upon arrival the room wasn't ready. Staff were friendly enough, but certainly didn't get any 'over and above' vibes. The grounds are run down and in need of maintenance and renovation. It is like an old abandoned amusement park. There is nothing to purchase, no drinks, no food. No staff to be seen.
Also you have to walk a million miles with luggage to get to your room. Or you can park in the back carpark, but don't expect staff to actually give you directions on how to get there.
The room is horrible. It is like a prison cell. No fresh air, and smells stale. The windows don't open, and I don't think it's ever had any fresh air through the place.
Carpets are stained and dirty with cobwebs in the corners.
One room is a small double bed with about 40cms each side. A bar fridge, a microwave and a tv. Nothing else. The other room is three sets of bunks with about 40cm between bunks. Again nothing else.
The bathroom is small, calling it clean is a stretch. You can see there has been poorly done DIY repair jobs over the years.
Internal walls are very thin and you can hear everything in other rooms.
We lasted literally 2 hours in there and left.
This is not worth the cost or the drive.
All the staff were very friendly and helpful
Rooms nice and clean plenty of space. We really enjoyed our visit plenty of time to walk round without the crowds. Staff very helpful if needed a great place for a few days downtime.

Self check in was easy, but no food options is disappointing, or even just a bar to get a drink.

The Abbey Tavern is there, one person could run it.

The whole place needs a boost of energy.

Room was more than adequate, but the bathroom needs a bit of work.
What an amazing experience!!! We will be back for sure my kids just loved the games room and play ground… thank you very much
There for the rodeo weekend great vibe definitely be back

Lovely modern cabin, comfy beds, huge shower head, mini kitchenette was handy. Very happy with stay apart from an oversight in housekeeping of cabin in between guests which was reported on departure. A couple of outdoor chairs on deck to enjoy the sun would be a good edition to cabin and provide seating option as no chairs in cabin likely due to space limitation.

The room was quite small but adequate. The air con was effective. When we checked in the room had one king bed and we had requested 2 single beds. Housekeeping quickly separated them into 2 single beds. Much appreciated. The beds and pillows were very comfy. The room was quiet despite the rodeo being on til midnight. We didn't hear a thing and were very grateful. I did think the price was rather a lot for the size of the room.

Great clean rooms. Lovely bathroom.  Nice simple breakfast. Lovely to walk around the grounds.  We stayed off peak, during the week, so nothing was open inside the grounds, but it was lovely to take photos and enjoy walking around the space in peace!
We would like to have seen brochures on local places to eat nearby, and activities that you can do outside of the castle.

We loved our family trip to Kryal Castle for the Halloween event! Staying in a castle was an exciting experience for the kids big and small and the staff were all fantastic! The continental breakfast and access to the grounds is great while staying. The sunset over Ballarat and foggy Kryal castle in the early morning was a worthwhile sight you wouldn’t see in normal visiting hours. The family room was very VERY small, so we probably will not stay overnight again, but we will return for future events.
Great to start the stay and visit with staying at Kryal castle

Loved loved loved staying in the castle. We stayed for the Ballarat Calisthenics Competition.  Made our time very relaxed.
Also loved the torture chamber and being able to walk around the grounds.

Good place to stay. Unfortunately, the rooms were not made up every day, leaving the floor messy and having to reuse towels and cutlery multiple times .
Staff were very friendly and helpful, basic amenities. The shower pressure was awful. All in all, it's a great place to stay and a very cool experience.
Such an amazing place to be at with family and friends, we absolutely loved our experience and time at Kryal Castle, it’s our third time here and looking forward to coming back again
Perfect accommodation for those visiting Kryal Castle. Extremely good value for money with entry and breakfast included. Clean rooms, friendly staff and a wonderful time.

On arrival the rooms were not clean to standard. Blood stained sheets. Opened alcohol cans, where one of my kids found. Made complaint however did not receive satisfactory response was informed that I would receive a call from management but did not receive call. They didn't even offer to give room a clean. Was not informed on arrival about the quest games in the castle on asking staff about them they did not no what quest games the castle offered. The grounds are kept to an OK level however could improve. The continental breakfast they offered they were always running out of food.
Unfortunately the accommodation was less than expected with cleanliness the main issue. Service was disappointing with lots of room for improvement.

Rooms are neat and tidy, however they could do with some touch ups. The king bed was beautiful to sleep in. The shower had no water pressure but the bathroom was spacious.
The grounds were kid friendly and it was great to be able to walk around before and after the park is open.

Hope we checked Google reviews before making the booking. We've had so many great experiences with Big4, hence booked this site straight away. Worst Big4 experience ever. Same as other reviews, facilities were usable but all had 'Out of Order' signs on. The staff lied about they were fully booked when we asked to upgrade. Kids had nothing to play. Very disappointing. DO NOT book your holiday here...

Don't book this big 4, it is a waste of money, The front desk staff completely lacked any intention to provide service. Despite the availability of upgraded camping sites, they insisted they were fully booked. Throughout our three-day stay, we didn't see anyone else checking in, yet they claimed to be a Big 4 brand. However, there were no Big 4 amenities; it was just a space with numerous safety hazards. The area was not child-friendly, and cleanliness was severely lacking. No staff members were cleaning the public toilets or replenishing toilet paper. If it doesn't meet Big 4 standards, it shouldn't open for business. The website only shows pictures with a "coming soon" caption, which is deceptive. This was one of the worst Big 4 experiences I've ever had.

We have just got back from 4 nights at this park across the Easter break. My advice - do not waste your money, there are better parks to stay at in the area. Not once across the 4 nights were the amenities cleaned, they put ‘Out of Order’ on 5 of the 10 cubicles and locked the disabled cubicle. Park was at about 85% capacity majority of the weekend and nil cleaning. We had to call the Castle and ask for toilet paper to be restocked on Sunday to which they only restocked the 5 cubicles that didn’t have the ‘Out of Order’ sign on them. Out of order cubicles were working totally fine and it appeared they didn’t want to use them as they’d have to then clean them but everyone continued to use them anyway. We had no hot water on first night to which we had to contact them about that too. Not much to do there apart from park your van up and sleep then get out of there as quick as you can. Some people were told they could have fire pits while others told no, still not sure who is correct. Staff were not pleasant to deal with on any occasion at all and even when the guy filled the toilet paper up he said his boss had yelled at him to do it.  Will not be returning to this park ever, very disappointed and can’t believe it’s a Big4, have notified Big4 HO of our experience due to their reputation. So many better options in area and better value for money, do not waste your time or money on this park.

Where do I start! The company made our experience great! Not the facilities, or there lack of! This should not be available to guests! It's not ready. Almost every toilet was marked as "out of order", the kitchen was marked "out of order" and there were building materials all over the grounds and there is nothing for kids to do. NOTHING. No BBQ. I am going to complain to BIG4 and tell anyone who will listen to me about my horrible experience. Completely misinformed. They didn't even have toilet paper. Not one person there was remotely happy about there stay.we lasted 2 nights out of the 3 we booked. NEVER AGAIN. GO THERE AT OWN RISK

We are staying here since 2 days in a powered camp site. The site is not ready for camping I am not sure why it is open to operation- there is construction stuff every where , rubbles, rusty iron stuff, sand etc. The kitchen is a joke it has one cook top, small fridge and ONE single sink for 100 people at a time to use. Most toilets and kitchen are labelled out of order (no toilet paper refilled) through out the day...exit gate doesn't work. No bbq area, no reception. We are big 4 members for many years now and we used other classic parks but this is the WORST experience  of all. I don't think it is worth the payment we made!

What an applaulling night. Book a room for a peaceful week end and they hosted a party where drunks sat outside my window yelling all night. No disclosure of an event. The night was a present from my sister and we sat there waiting for it to stop as couldn’t even hear the telly. What the hell.

My sister and I booked a cabin for an event in March, via the Big4 site. At the time of booking, there was a minimum of 3 cabins available on our selected dates. The following day my sister received a call saying that we no longer had accommodation as the cabin had been double booked. She was told it was because we used a third-party booking site such as booking .com – which was incorrect. I then attempted to call 20+ times – no answer, so resorted to leaving a message. When I finally spoke to someone an hour later, she argued with what was said to my sister, said Big4 was not a large, well-known organization, threatened to hang up if I didn’t ‘calm down’ and offered “these things happen” as an explanation for the booking error. Given the size of the Big4 network (180 locations nationwide), you’d think that they would have a working online booking system, but apparently not. We have since booked accommodation at Woodmanshill Motel and Caravan Park – where the booking process was seamless, and communication has been fantastic!

If I could negative stars I would. We booked a few months ago, as we were planning our trip around VIC. (We're from mid North Coast NSW).
6 days before we were due to check in we had a phone call cancelling our booking due to conflict with the council over permits. 6 days... before our check in on Boxing Day.
No compassion, no real apology and no help to find alternate accommodation for the time we had booked.
Luckily enough we were able to book with the NRMA Park Ballarat, who were extremely helpful, and accommodating for our dog (not allowed during peak, but we were able to bring her).
I'm sure Big 4 Kryal Castle knew long before the 6 days notice we were given. Disgusting.

We planned a family trip with our 3-year-old daughter to BIG4 Kryal Castle for the last week of December. We booked it two months early, but just a few days before, they called us. They said they couldn't let us stay because of missing some approval. This was really upsetting because we were all looking forward to it, especially my daughter.

It was our Christmas holiday, and now it was ruined. We tried to find another place, but it was too late to book anything else. It's really sad how our whole plan got messed up because of their mistake.

I can't believe a big place like this made such a big error. It's not fair to us and the other families. We were all so disappointed. This was supposed to be a special holiday, but it turned out really bad.

Just got informed that our accommodation for next week after Christmas is cancelled
We booked months ago
Read recent reviews and apparently they closed the park over a month ago

This would be considered fraudulent to accept payment knowing you won't be open

Now we have to wait don't know how long for a refund to be processed
It was great, we enjoyed our stay can't wait to see the swimming pool put in

Worst booking experience I have ever had. Husband and I booked one of the tiny homes for this past weekend. We are reenactors and had an obligation to attend Kryal's Victorian Medieval Festival as part of the entertainment. We booked our room months in advance and did not find out Kryal Big 4 had cancelled our booking until my husband called them the day before our arrival. I found a post on their Facebook page from over a week ago saying they were closing the Big 4 Caravan park. Friends of ours had actually received a text message to inform them of their cancellation two days out from the event. Fortunately my husband was able to sort some last minute accommodation in Ballarat but this left us out of pocket $450.

I felt that I was completely ripped off and very dissatisfied with the lack of facilities for the amount I was charged per night. A camp kitchen that couldn't be accessed until it was finished.  We came here to relax and enjoy our time to be woken up at 7.30am by tradies and their nail guns going off.  The constant opening of toilet doors (which can be opened from the outside) by kids running around whilst having a shower was quite disconcerting.The fact that there was never anyone around when needed and the toilets needed to be cleaned more than one a day. Very overrated.
A lovely new park will be even better once finished.
Great spot next door to Castle. Fee includes entrance to Castle! Some features still to be developed.

Terrible rooms.  We booked a 4 sleeper cabin not realising that it was 2 separate cabins. We stated that our kids were under 8 and no one advised us that this was the layout. So if you're willing to leave young children unsupervised in a cabin that you can't get into quickly you and your other half are sleeping in separate rooms.
The rooms themselves are cramped, only big enough for the bed. There is no other furniture or even somewhere to put your suitcase.
We had water pooling on the floors before even getting in due to the rain and the fact the doors lack any type of weather strip which was allowing more water in.
Luckily the duty manager brought us more towels to mop up and seal the bottom of the doors.  He also was kind enough to bring extra cutlery as whoever does the cleaning, missed knives, forks and glasses. Nothing worse than finding the previous guests' dirty cutlery and glasses in your room.
The weather was terrible overnight. One of cabin doors kept banging like someone was trying to break in and the exhaust fan in the bathroom was rattling all night.
We were given a complimentary buffet breakfast for the experience which sounded fair at the time but in reality was almost a sick joke.
You make your way into the castle's abbey for breakfast. This was exciting as your memories of old mediaeval movies start to play in your mind.
It was 3 degrees outside and maybe 4 degrees inside. The only fire going were the tiny burners under the pityful excuse for a buffet. We had the 9am sitting. Got in at 9.10 to find people in beanies, down filled vests and full goretex with their breath visible, probably wondering why no one bothered to light the fire in the fireplace. Even if the physics of radiant heat wasn't going to efficiently heat up the abbey it could have given the diners some respite. The food at 9.10 had all but gone. A few cooked up frozen hash browns returning to their initial frigid state, some cold bacon, fried eggs and tomato.
The untrained staff hid behind the bar totally relying on the diners to inform them of the lack of food or juice.
They were, however, only too happy to nuke a diner's plate of cold food and did make a decent coffee which albeit cooled down within the first 2 minutes to a temperature you'd be comfortable giving to a 3 year old.
The fact that this would cost $80 for a family of 4 is criminal.
Dare I say it, but the Macca's breakfast is hotter, fresher and more inviting at half the price and you can leave the goretex in the car.
The place has a lot of potential but the staff need training and the cabins need maintenance despite looking less than 5 years old.
I can't recommend this place to anyone other than those using their own caravans or tradie couples who might be able to do some free repairs during their stay. The fact that at this time castle entry is also included is a small win. Such a pity.
